SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026

sources: SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026
Zycus with Omnia: Modules Walkthrough, 18 Mar 2026
metrics.narrative: The buyer's pain is quantified: manual, Excel-based contract governance was covering a spend base large enough that the negotiated SOW raised the contractually-covered threshold from ZAR 900 million to ZAR 1 billion across 20 contract subtypes with no added cost to Zycus (SOW review and closure calls, 23 and 30 Jun 2026). Spend-visibility lag (monthly refresh, described as too infrequent) was also raised as a pain point earlier in the walkthrough sessions (18 Mar 2026).

sources: SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026
champion.narrative: Marianne Ventura has personally driven the entire closing sequence -- reviewing and negotiating SOW redlines, absorbing scope growth without asking for a price change, coordinating the internal legal-cover signature, and stating the buyer's own urgency to sign within days. This is strong, active advocacy with real internal authority, not a developing relationship.

competition.narrative: No active named commercial rival appears anywhere in the record at this late stage. The genuine competitive tension is inertia -- the buyer's own manual, Excel-based governance -- plus a brief, long-since-faded look at other S2C vendors early in the cycle that was never revisited once the SOW work began.

sources: SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and buyer-articulated: reliance on manual, Excel-based governance over a large and growing spend base, infrequent spend-data refresh, and a compliance gap around South Africa-specific sanctions and politically-exposed-person screening that the buyer flagged as unresolved even at SOW-closure stage.

sources: SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026
paper_process.narrative: As of the 30 Jun 2026 closure call, the SOW redlines were resolved (country phasing, material-breach definition, demonstrated-functionality language), Certinal was confirmed for e-signature, and the agreed sequence was: Marianne Ventura completes Omnia's internal legal-cover signature, then routes the MSA, Order Forms, and SOW to Zycus for countersignature by its Global Delivery signatory. No confirmation exists yet that this sequence has completed.

sources: Account contact role: Jacques De Villiers, Group Head of Supply Chain
economic_buyer.narrative: Two individuals were named by the champion as the required budget approvers as far back as the demo-prep call on 8 Oct 2025: an unnamed Omnia legal director (also a company director) and Jacques de Villiers (Group Head of Supply Chain). Marianne Ventura described her own task at that time as working to 'get him to actually sign the money' -- indicating EB engagement has run through the champion rather than direct Zycus-to-EB contact. Neither has been named again in the record since October 2025, so engagement is real but indirect and stale.

sources: SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026
decision_process.narrative: The process is well documented end to end: alignment/demo (Oct 2025) to SOW scoping (Feb 2026) to legal alignment on enforceability (17 Jun 2026) to SOW redline review (23 Jun 2026) to SOW closure (30 Jun 2026), with signature routed first through Omnia's internal legal cover and then to Zycus's countersigning authority.

sources: Source to Contract Scope of Work for Finalization, 19 Feb 2026
decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria were worked through in detail across the SOW sessions: 20 contract subtypes, approval workflows, supplier-onboarding parallel processing, DNB/EcoVadis/VVT validation integrations, and a data migration covering roughly 6,000 contracts and suppliers on a source-agnostic basis. The buyer's legal side also required that demonstrated product functionality be captured as an enforceable contract term.

items.name: Omnia<>Zycus catch up
items.summary: Brief morning call ahead of the SOW closure session; not recorded, content unknown.

items.name: SOW discussion and closure call
items.summary: All SOW redlines resolved; spend threshold raised to ZAR 1B with no commercial uplift; Certinal confirmed as e-sign method; buyer to complete internal legal sign-off and route documents for countersignature.

day_summary.overall: On 30 Jun 2026 (~9 days before this sweep), Zycus and Omnia held two calls: an early morning catch-up (not recorded, content unknown) and a detailed SOW discussion and closure call in which every outstanding redline was resolved — country phasing adjusted, spend threshold raised to ZAR 1 billion with no commercial uplift, and Certinal confirmed as the e-signature method. Omnia's champion committed to completing internal legal sign-off and routing the SOW/MSA/Order Forms to Zycus for countersignature, saying the team needed to 'report we're ready to sign' within days. No further contact has been logged since.

cro_panel.intro: This Opportunity record was created only 3 days before this sweep (6 Jul 2026) as an annual-invoicing tracking shell, but it carries the full S2C24 module scope whose underlying deal reached full SOW agreement and a stated buyer intent to sign as of 30 Jun 2026, roughly 9 days before this sweep.; No Salesforce activity, Next Step, or contact roles have been logged directly against this record, and no contact is confirmed since that call

blocks.read: Late-stage by the book, but the signals underneath are thin.

bullets.text: Salesforce close date (2 Jul 2027) is roughly a year past the actual expected signature window, risking a stale forecast if not corrected.
Partner-vs-Zycus change-management staffing is still undecided; Marianne said she didn't understand why a partner was originally scoped in.

blocks.read: Going quiet — engagement has dropped off.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No active rival vendor is in play.

bullets.text: Spend threshold and config scope both grew (ZAR 900M to 1B; 15 to 20 contract subtypes) with no commercial uplift, trimming margin on an already-negotiated deal.
Recorded close date (2 Jul 2027) is roughly a year past the buyer's own stated signing window, which was imminent as of 30 Jun 2026.

cro_panel.header: Omnia Holdings Limited — Vendor Selected · $234K · closes 2 Jun

contributions.detail: CloseDate is roughly 12 months past the buyer's own stated signing window — high stale-forecast risk on a Best Case deal.
Zero meetings logged at any horizon and an empty Next Step/History — no documented forward motion for a Vendor Selected opportunity.
Undecided partner staffing plus scope growth without commercial uplift add execution and margin risk.
Mitigants: no active competitor, MEDDPICC almost fully confirmed, and a demonstrably engaged champion keep loss-risk lower than the process risk alone would suggest.

deal_risk.text: CloseDate is roughly 12 months past the buyer's own stated signing window — high stale-forecast risk on a Best Case deal.

win_position.text: Vendor Selected with no active rival — deal is displacing Omnia's manual Excel process; a prior mention of alternative vendors (Apr 2025) never resurfaced.
Champion Marianne Ventura personally owns resolving every SOW redline and has committed to completing internal sign-off herself — strong buyer-side ownership, not just a rep plan.
Buyer proactively asked to add Merlin Insights (AI spend analysis) into the SOW on top of Merlin Intake/Contract Discovery/Risk Radar already demoed — real, buyer-initiated expansion.
MEDDPICC marks Economic Buyer only 'partial' — no confirmed direct EB face time evidenced.
Recorded close date (Jul 2027) is ~a year past the buyer's own signing window (imminent as of 30 Jun 2026) — the record, not the deal, looks stale.
No Next Step and no logged meeting of any kind exist for this account despite live SOW redlining — a visibility gap at the finish line.

deal_momentum.text: Zero meetings recorded at any horizon (30d/60d/90d/all-time) for this account — no logged cadence despite Vendor Selected stage.
Next_Step__c is blank and Next_Step_History__c is null — no dated forward plan on file.
Champion-led SOW redlines (design-document incorporation, material-breach protections) were worked Jan–Jun 2026 — real recent legal progress, just not captured as a 'meeting.'
CloseDate (2 Jul 2027) has drifted roughly 12 months past the buyer's own stated signing window — a major forecast-hygiene drag.
No future meeting/event scheduled — nothing forward-dated to anchor momentum right when signature should be near.

Scope grew (ZAR 900M→1B threshold; 15→20 contract subtypes) with no matching commercial uplift, and no reference-check or procurement-review activity is evidenced.

fit.ai_fit_signal: Omnia adopted several AI-enabled modules into scope without resistance and asked detailed questions about scoring methodology and AI-driven bid evaluation, but never framed the deal as AI-first.
fit.expectations_fit: Fit is strong on the core S2C suite and integration architecture; the two open exposures are spend-refresh frequency and the sanctions-screening tool, both explicitly deferred to the design phase.
fit.positioning_strength: Zycus's Merlin AI layer was demonstrated in depth (Merlin Intake, Merlin contract discovery, Merlin Risk Radar, Questionnaire Genie), and the buyer proactively asked to add Merlin Insights (AI spend analysis) into the SOW rather than needin

evidence_packet.champion: Marianne Ventura has personally carried this deal from business-case defense through SOW closure, taking ownership of resolving every redline and committing to complete internal sign-off herself.

competition.summary: No active rival vendor is in play. The deal is displacing Omnia's own manual, Excel-based governance; a generic mention of unnamed alternative vendors from April 2025 was never repeated, and legacy internal ERP systems were treated as scope exclusions, not competitors.

scope_change.to: ZAR 1 billion spend threshold / 20 contract subtypes / DRC and Indonesia excluded (legacy ERP systems AX2012/QAD), Mauritius rephased to Africa phase 2, Brazil rephased to Americas
scope_change.from: ZAR 900 million spend threshold / 15 contract subtypes / broader geography including DRC and Indonesia
scope_change.detail: Net contractually-covered footprint grew in threshold and subtype coverage but trimmed two markets blocked by legacy ERP systems, with no commercial uplift to Zycus.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Merlin AI capabilities were covered in the modules walkthrough and business case sessions, but the buyer's own asks centered on data migration, integration, and compliance tooling rather than AI-specific requests.

business_case.evidence: Buyer agreed to raise the contractually-covered spend threshold to ZAR 1 billion across 20 contract subtypes with no additional commercial ask from Zycus, and pushed for demonstrated functionality to be contractually enforceable (SOW review and alignment calls, 17 and 23 Jun 2026).

items.change: Opportunity created (Vendor Selected / Best Case, $234,000, close date 2 Jul 2027) with no independent activity of its own

deal_movement.summary: This opportunity was created on 6 Jul 2026 as the annual-invoicing tracker following the underlying deal's SOW closure; the substantive negotiation concluded 8 days before this sweep with no independent update yet logged against this record.

forecast_read.math: Opportunity created 2026-07-06 (2 days before this sweep) with close date 2027-07-02 = 359 days out; last verified account-level buyer engagement was 2026-06-30, 8 days prior to this sweep, at which point signature was described as imminent.
forecast_read.reason: The recorded close date (2 Jul 2027) sits roughly a year past the buyer's own stated signing timeline: on the last verified call (30 Jun 2026, 8 days before this sweep), the champion said 'we've gotta go in a couple of days, and we have to report we're ready to sign' after the SOW redlines closed. This specific opportunity record has no independent Next Step, contact role, or activity of its own to confirm what happened since, so Best Case with this close date is very likely a stale placeholder rather than a considered forecast.

items.detail: Final SOW redlines around design-document incorporation and material-breach protections took from Jan to Jun 2026 to resolve due to external counsel delays; now closed but shows Omnia's legal caution.
Salesforce close date (2 Jul 2027) is roughly a year past the actual expected signature window, risking a stale forecast if not corrected.
Partner-vs-Zycus change-management staffing is still undecided; Marianne said she didn't understand why a partner was originally scoped in.
Spend threshold and config scope both grew (ZAR 900M to 1B; 15 to 20 contract subtypes) with no commercial uplift, trimming margin on an already-negotiated deal.
Recorded close date (2 Jul 2027) is roughly a year past the buyer's own stated signing window, which was imminent as of 30 Jun 2026.
SOW redline resolution ran from Feb through Jun 2026 due to external counsel delays; now resolved, but shows the buyer's legal process is slow and cautious.
Whether a partner or Zycus itself staffs change management for rollout remains undecided.
Contractually-covered spend threshold and contract-subtype count both grew during closure with no commercial uplift, trimming margin on an already modest $234,000 deal.
Sanctions and politically-exposed-person screening approach for South Africa (World-Check One vs DNB/Refinitiv/iOxcio) is unresolved and deferred to implementation.

critical_signals.text: The most recent verified conversation, 8 days before this sweep, closed out the statement of work with the buyer's own champion saying they needed to be ready to sign within days -- yet nothing has confirmed since whether that happened.

During closure, the buyer's covered spend threshold and contract-type count both grew and two countries were carved out for a legacy system limitation, all with no increase to the deal's value, quietly trimming margin on an already modest contract.

The buyer wants a lower-cost alternative to its current sanctions and politically-exposed-person screening tool for South Africa, but that decision was pushed to implementation rather than settled before signature.
No rival vendor is in the picture at this stage; the only real competitor left standing is the buyer's own habit of manual, spreadsheet-based governance if momentum stalls before ink is on paper.

confidence_signals.summary: Evidence for the underlying deal is rich and recent through 30 Jun 2026, but this specific opportunity record carries none of its own activity, next step, or contact roles, which caps confidence in the current read.

north_star_verdict.math: SF close_date is 360 days out (2 Jul 2027); CreatedDate is 2026-07-06 (this opp record is one day old, likely tracking a future annual-invoicing checkpoint rather than the contract execution event). Avoma evidence shows the actual contracting timeline is essentially complete: SOW finalization began 19 Feb 2026, ran through legal redlines to 17 Jun 2026, and fully closed out on 30 Jun 2026 with signature mechanics agreed. The true expected execution date is days-to-weeks away, roughly a year ahead of the recorded close_date.
evidence: SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026: 'we've cleared the SOW... I look forward to getting it back' (Zycus) / 'we've gotta go in a couple of days, and we have to report we're ready to sign' (Marianne Ventura)
Signature routing agreed 30 Jun 2026: Marianne to complete internal legal-cover (Adobe Sign) then route to Zycus's named AVP Global Delivery signatory
north_star_verdict.headline: This is a fully negotiated, near-signature deal whose recorded close date (2 Jul 2027) does not reflect reality: the underlying SOW/MSA was finalized and signature routing agreed on the 30 Jun 2026 call, seven days before this sweep, with the buyer's champion saying she expects to report the deal ready to sign within days.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Commit (contract fully negotiated, signature routing already agreed; recorded close_date needs correction to reflect near-term execution rather than a placeholder a year out)

flags: Lock in Opportunity Contact Roles for Marianne Ventura, Jacques de Villiers, Tracy Seeley-Jeppe, Dieter Sparbier, and Zama Khambula now, before the deal hands off to delivery, so the buying committee isn't lost at signature.
Confirm the identity of the board-level executive Marianne referenced approving budget (17 Jun 2026) so post-signature expansion and renewal conversations have a mapped executive sponsor.
Use the imminent signature as leverage to close the open Dun & Bradstreet ($10-25k/yr) enrichment and full iRisk upsell conversations before budget attention moves to delivery.
Confirm signature status now: the buyer stated intent to sign 'in a couple of days' as of 30 Jun 2026; 8 days have passed with nothing logged, making a direct check-in the top priority.
best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-value move is confirming whether the buyer's stated signing intent from 8 days ago actually happened; the record itself also needs basic hygiene to reflect a deal this advanced.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer voiced explicit urgency to sign within days immediately after accepting Zycus's proposed SOW terms (SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026).

competitive_position.summary: No active named commercial rival appears at this late stage. The real competitive tension is inertia -- the buyer's own manual, Excel-based governance -- with a brief, long-faded look at other S2C vendors early in the cycle.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This Opportunity record was created only 3 days before this sweep (6 Jul 2026) as an annual-invoicing tracking shell, but it carries the full S2C24 module scope whose underlying deal reached full SOW agreement and a stated buyer intent to sign as of 30 Jun 2026, roughly 9 days before this sweep. No Salesforce activity, Next Step, or contact roles have been logged directly against this record, and no contact is confirmed since that call.

deal_risk.text: Contract scope grew (ZAR 900M to 1B spend threshold; 15 to 20 contract subtypes) during closure with no commercial uplift, trimming margin on an already-modest $234,000 deal.
The recorded Close Date (2 Jul 2027) sits roughly a year past the buyer's own stated imminent signing window (30 Jun 2026), making the Best Case forecast on this date indefensible until corrected.
Sanctions/PEP screening tooling for South Africa remains unresolved and deferred to the design phase, alongside an undecided partner-vs-Zycus change-management staffing question.

win_position.text: By 30 Jun 2026, Omnia's champion confirmed all SOW redlines resolved, the spend threshold and contract-subtype scope expanded with no additional Zycus commercial ask, and Certinal locked in as the e-signature method, stating 'we've gotta go in a couple of days, and we have to report we're ready to sign.'

The Zycus AVP Global Delivery signatory chain was confirmed on the same call, and the champion committed to completing Omnia's internal legal-cover signature before routing documents for countersignature.

No Salesforce activity, Next Step, or Opportunity Contact Role has been logged against this specific record, and there is no confirmation that the signature described as 'a couple of days' away on 30 Jun 2026 has actually happened in the 9 days since.

Reach into the economic buyer layer (the Omnia legal director, Jacques de Villiers) has only ever been indirect through the champion since their single joint appearance on 8 Oct 2025; no direct Zycus engagement with either is on record.

deal_momentum.text: Ten buyer touchpoints in the 90 days before this sweep (17 Jun, 23 Jun, 30 Jun x2 2026) show accelerating, substantive two-way engagement culminating in a stated intent to sign.
Zero contact from either side has been logged in the 9 days since the 30 Jun 2026 closure call, and this Opportunity record itself was created on 6 Jul 2026 with no Next Step yet defined.

customer_commitment.text: Marianne Ventura has driven the deal from a business-case defense on 8 Oct 2025 through SOW closure, personally committing to complete Omnia's internal legal sign-off.
Commitment runs almost entirely through a single champion; the economic buyer and the legal director have not been directly engaged by Zycus since 8 Oct 2025.
